factors.name: US Inflation Surge (Oil-Driven)
factors.summary: Treasury yields climbing on persistent inflation concerns; WTI holding near $97.50 as Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed. Bloomberg reports global bond yields rising amid inflation angst and energy shock.

factors.name: Fed Policy Ambiguity
factors.summary: Fed's Barr and Williams remain noncommittal on June/future hikes; Barr hasn't decided on June FOMC move. Policy described as 'in a good place' but inflation uncertainty noted as key risk.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength (DXY >99.00)
factors.summary: DXY climbs above 99.00 for fifth consecutive day on strong retail sales (+0.5% MoM) and Fed hawkish shift. Headwind for Dow multinationals with ~50% international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Trade Summit (Positive Optics)
factors.summary: China agrees to buy 200 Boeing jets and double-digit billions in US agricultural products. Trade truce extends, tariff uncertainty remains but tone constructive. USTR Greer signals 'lot of success' in rebalancing.

factors.name: Boeing Disappointment (Dow Component)
factors.summary: Boeing stock headed for steepest 6-month drop as China order (200 jets) fell far short of expectations (500+). Price-weighted Dow hit hard by individual heavyweight underperformance.

factors.name: Global Growth Weakness & Supply Chain Stress
factors.summary: South Korea's KOSPI reversed from 8,046 record high to -2% on Iran/Hormuz concerns. New Zealand manufacturing PMI collapsed to 50.5 from 52.8; freight disruptions cited. Iran war resolution at impasse.

factors.name: Japan Wholesale Inflation Shock (PPI +4.9% YoY)
factors.summary: Japan April PPI surged 4.9% vs 3.0% expected (largest since 2014), import prices up 17.5% YoY on Iran war energy costs. Supports BOJ June rate hike case; global inflation pressure broadening.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran, Hormuz Closure)
factors.summary: Trump signals impatience with Iran; Strait of Hormuz effectively closed. Oil elevated but risk premium embedded; uncertainty on resolution weighing on cyclical confidence and shipping indices.

factors.name: Dow Earnings Mix / Forward Guidance Pending
factors.summary: Limited component earnings data in this window. Achieve Life Sciences beat Q1 EPS, but no major Dow heavyweights (UNH, JPM, CAT, GS, HD) reported. Guidance will be critical test for cyclical outlook.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising (Rate Hikes Priced In)
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yields climbing on inflation and Fed uncertainty. Higher real yields typically headwind for equities, particularly dividend-heavy Dow; rate-sensitive financials face compression.

factors.name: AI Sector Momentum Fading
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports 'Record-breaking global stock rally driven by AI optimism stalled in Asia; sentiment souring.' Nvidia earnings expected but sentiment already cooling; Nvidia a top Dow/broader influence.

factors.name: Copper Retreat & Metals Weakness
factors.summary: Copper extending retreat from record highs as inflation and stronger dollar weigh. Industrial input cost relief fading; bad signal for CAT, DE, MMM guidance and capex cycles.

screening.narrative: The Dow faces cross-currents: Trump-Xi trade optimism (China ag + Boeing orders) is offset by broadening global inflation shock (Japan PPI +4.9%, Treasuries rising, dollar strong), supply chain stress from Iran/Hormuz closure, and fading AI/growth momentum. Boeing's disappointing order miss has dampened index enthusiasm. Fed policy ambiguity (Barr/Williams sitting on hands into June) and climbing real yields are headwinds for cyclicals and dividend payers. The 0.12% 4h / 0.23% 24h move is muted, suggesting consolidation rather than conviction in either direction.
catalysts_ahead: US Retail Sales (already released; +0.5% MoM beat, bullish signal for consumers but stoked inflation fears)
ISM Manufacturing PMI (due Fri–Mon window; critical for Dow industrial exposure)
ISM Services PMI (due Fri–Mon window; services-heavy Dow exposure test)
Dow component earnings (UNH, JPM, GS, HD, CAT, V, MSFT, BA—forward guidance critical)
FOMC Meeting / Dot Plot (mid-May; rate-expectations hinge point)
China macro data (latest PMI, industrial output; affects CAT, BA demand)
CPI/PCE inflation prints (May releases; will confirm or deny peak-inflation narrative)
Japan BOJ action (Jun rate hike likely; yen intervention risk)
Geopolitical resolution on Iran/Hormuz (if resolved, relief rally; if escalates, oil spike)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed signals imminent rate cuts (risk-off reversal into safe havens), (2) Iran conflict resolves and oil drops sharply (inflation fears ease, real yields fall), (3) Dow heavyweights (CAT, JPM, GS) deliver upside earnings + bullish guidance (cyclical rebound), (4) China PMI or stimulus surprises to upside (rescues growth narrative), or (5) Trump-Xi tariff deal is fully resolved with zero new tariffs (trade risk-off fully priced).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is mixed and early-stage; market consolidating between competing narratives (trade optimism vs. inflation/rate concerns). Await ISM PMI and first earnings set (JPM, GS, UNH) before committing. Short resistance at 50,200–50,400 on rate-rise concerns; long support at 49,800 if inflation narrative softens. Confidence limited by conflicting signals and undecided Fed stance—do not chase either direction.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Summit & Trade Optimism
factors.summary: Trump claimed 'fantastic trade deals' with China including agricultural purchases, Boeing orders (200 jets), and oil commitments. However, Boeing disappointment (expected 500+) and modest specificity temper upside. Market initially rallied Thursday but sentiment has already shifted risk-off Friday.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Cost Shock
factors.summary: WTI surging to ~$98.50, Iran tensions unresolved, Strait of Hormuz closure risks. Japan wholesale prices +4.9% YoY (April), far above 3.0% forecast; naphtha +83.2% MoM. Oil prices crimp margins for Dow industrials (CAT, BA, transport costs) and raise stagflation risk.

factors.name: USD Strength & DXY Rally
factors.summary: DXY trading above 99.00 for fifth consecutive day on strong retail sales (+0.5% MoM) and Fed rate-hike bets. USD/JPY at two-week highs, EUR/USD at five-week lows. Strong dollar benefits US multinationals' competitiveness but headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue.

factors.name: Fed Rate-Hike Expectations & Yield Pressure
factors.summary: Bond yields surging to near one-year highs amid inflation data (retail sales beat, PPI spikes). Fed's Barr (May 15) said he hasn't decided June FOMC action but acknowledged energy shock impact. Rate-sensitive Dow names (utilities, dividend payers) under pressure; financial NII support offset by valuation compression.

factors.name: Asian Equity Contagion & Risk-Off Reversal
factors.summary: South Korea's KOSPI reversed from all-time 8,045+ high to -2%+ decline as Trump signaled impatience on Iran. Taiwan Weighted -1.25%. Australia ASX 200 -0.11%. Asia weakness Friday morning (local time) + Nasdaq futures -1.2% suggest risk-off wave ahead of US premarket open.

factors.name: Nasdaq Weakness & Tech Sector Headwinds
factors.summary: Nasdaq futures down ~1.2% on 'sell-the-news' post-Trump-Xi summit and crypto weakness. Nvidia earnings looming; MarketWatch noted overbought warnings and sell signals on S&P 500. Dow is less tech-heavy, but cross-market weakness is contagious.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings & Forward Guidance Gap
factors.summary: Honda +7% on restructuring hope despite 70-year operating loss, signaling M&A/restructuring upside. Boeing disappointed on China order size. Limited fresh earnings catalysts in immediate window; most of Dow's big names (UNH, GS, JPM, CAT) not reporting in next 24-48h.

factors.name: China Growth & PMI Signals Unclear
factors.summary: Trump touted China market opening in stages, but no formal growth data released. PBOC set USD/CNY at 6.8415 vs. 6.7976 estimate, modest yuan weakness. China manufacturing/services PMI not yet released for May; prior month data not in news window.

factors.name: Geopolitical Uncertainty: Iran, Middle East, Taiwan
factors.summary: Iran war still raging; US-Israel strikes reported; Strait of Hormuz closure risk persists. Trump patience on Iran waning per Fox interview. Taiwan policy ambiguity (no explicit commitment to defend). These tail risks support higher oil, VIX, and defensive positioning.

factors.name: Immediate Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Dow +0.12% in 4h, +0.23% in 24h — minimal move, suggesting indecision. Overnight rally (+0.23%) fades into Asian/European session. No clear breakout; consolidation pattern near all-time highs (Dow just below record territory Thursday).

screening.narrative: The Dow opened Friday on a cautious note despite Trump's China trade announcements. While the 'fantastic trade deals' narrative initially supported equities Thursday, Friday's session has been dominated by risk-off sentiment as Nasdaq futures plunged -1.2%, Asian equities reversed (KOSPI -2%), and oil rallied on Iran tensions and Hormuz closure fears. Energy cost shocks (Japan PPI +4.9%, naphtha +83.2% MoM) and rising real yields (bond yields to near one-year highs) are pressuring dividend payers and industrials. The strong dollar (+0.5% retail sales, DXY >99) is a mixed blessing for Dow multinationals. Sentiment has shifted from 'deal optimism' to 'stagflation concerns' within hours.
catalysts_ahead: US ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (May, likely later this week) — critical for industrial-heavy Dow composition
Fed speakers & rate-hike pricing (Barr signaled uncertainty on June; market is pricing ~70% chance of hike by July)
Oil price moves & Hormuz updates (geopolitical wildcard)
China economic data (manufacturing PMI, official PMI release if due)
Dow heavyweight earnings: Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Bank of America potential in coming weeks
Treasury auction calendar & 10-year yield trajectory (currently pressuring dividend payers)
screening.risk_warning: A swift resolution to Iran tensions or explicit US-China tariff truce could flip the bias to BULLISH. Conversely, a sharp oil spike (>$105 WTI) or Fed hawkish surprise could trigger cascading selling, breaking key support. The current NEUTRAL stance reflects high crosscurrents: Trump deal-making optimism vs. energy shock stagflation fears vs. tight Fed policy. Watch the close for direction; if risk-off persists into cash market, support break below 49,800 is a material bearish signal.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (Asian weakness spreading to premarket). Avoid aggressive entries at current levels. If Dow bounces back above 50,200 on US cash open, look for a retest of 50,350 resistance; break above = potential continuation. If it rolls over and closes below 49,900, a deeper pullback to 49,600–49,700 is likely — better entry zone for contrarian longs. Short-term bias is too mixed for confident directional conviction.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: US inflation data (retail sales, CPI) triggered hawkish Fed repricing; Treasury yields jumped across the curve with 10Y near one-year highs. Dollar rallying hard on rate hike bets, which pressures equities.

factors.name: Bond Yield Surge
factors.summary: 10Y yields extend higher, 30Y broke 5% threshold earlier in week. Rising long-end yields compress valuations on dividend payers and rate-sensitive Dow components (utilities, financials).

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: Dollar heading for best week since March per Bloomberg; EUR/USD broke below 50/200-day MAs (now ~1.1630, 5-week low). Strong dollar headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Cost Inflation
factors.summary: WTI surged above $100 on Trump-China crude purchase talk and Middle East supply fears (Strait of Hormuz tensions). Higher energy input costs pressure industrial margins; CVX benefit offset by broader cost drag.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Summit Outcome
factors.summary: Summit concluded with 'few major breakthroughs' per Bloomberg and Al Jazeera; choreographed but lacking substantive trade deals or Iran/Hormuz breakthrough. Risk-off momentum as markets digest lack of concrete wins.

factors.name: Equity Futures Weakness
factors.summary: Dow futures fell 0.35% below 50k in European hours; S&P 500 futures -0.56% to ~7,480; Nasdaq -0.81% to ~29,450 per FXStreet. 'Sell the news' after Wall Street record highs; profit-taking visible.

factors.name: AI-Led Earnings Strength
factors.summary: BNY Mellon notes US equities propelled by 'unusually strong, AI-led earnings boom outpacing broader economy.' This is supporting index gains despite macro headwinds; Microsoft stake by Ackman/Pershing Square signals mega-cap AI confidence.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran/Hormuz)
factors.summary: Middle East tensions persist; Strait of Hormuz disruption fears drive oil but also broader uncertainty. UAE accelerating alternative pipeline to 2027, but near-term supply shock risk remains unresolved.

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings Guides
factors.summary: No major Dow heavyweight earnings revisions in today's news; Goldman Sachs raised targets on Kodiak Gas, Viking Holdings (niche names). Lack of broad guidance update limits conviction.

factors.name: Cass Freight / Real Economy Signals
factors.summary: No fresh Cass Freight, rail, or port data in overnight window. Real-economy strength/weakness unclear; limits macro certainty.

factors.name: Adani Settlement (India/Emerging Markets)
factors.summary: US dropping criminal case, Adani settling civil for $18M; reduces tail risk but no direct Dow exposure. Minor positive for emerging market sentiment but negligible for US30.

factors.name: VIX & Credit Spreads
factors.summary: Risk-off wave noted at open (investinglive); broader sentiment turning defensive as yields spike and summit yields no deals. Implied vol likely rising into close-of-week chop.

screening.narrative: The Dow opened Friday on the back of record closes, but a risk-off reversal has unfolded as markets digest a hawkish repricing of Fed rate hikes following hot US inflation data and strong retail sales. Treasury yields surged, the dollar rallied to its best week in two months, and the Trump-Xi summit failed to deliver substantive trade breakthroughs—triggering 'sell the news' momentum in futures (Dow futures -0.35%, S&P 500 -0.56%, Nasdaq -0.81%). Oil climbed on supply fears but energy cost inflation is a headwind for industrial margins. AI-led earnings strength (especially mega-cap tech) is one of few bullish anchors, offsetting cyclical pressure from higher real yields and dollar strength hitting multinational revenue translation.
catalysts_ahead: Friday US stock close (final 3h): profit-taking vs AI/earnings bid—direction unclear
Weekly close (tonight): technical setup for next week; potential pivot if yields stabilize
FOMC meeting (mid-May or early June, if scheduled): hawkish repricing may be ahead of curve
ISM Manufacturing PMI & Services PMI (next week): test for economic resilience amid rate hike fears
Dow component earnings (next 1-2 weeks): UNH, JPM, GS guidance will heavily influence index
China manufacturing PMI / China policy (late May): growth slowdown risk; key for CAT, BA, materials demand
screening.risk_warning: If Treasury yields pull back sharply (e.g., Fed officials hint patience on rate hikes, or recession data spike), the bearish dollar and lower-yield headwinds reverse, and AI mega-caps re-accelerate, the Dow could pivot back to bullish. Conversely, if profit-taking extends and real yields stay elevated, support may break lower. Middle East escalation could also trigger a risk-off flush below 49,800.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in profit-taking (PHASE_2); wait for either a retest of 50,300–50,500 resistance or a flush to 49,800–49,900 support before entering fresh positions. High near-term chop expected into week-end; conviction remains low (4/10) due to mixed drivers (AI strength vs rate/dollar headwinds) and move-in-progress status.

factors.name: Treasury Yields / Rate Expectations
factors.summary: 10Y yields hit near one-year highs; 30Y broke 5% threshold earlier in week. Bond market repricing hawkish Fed expectations amid inflation concerns. Rising yields pressure dividend-heavy Dow names and cap valuations.

factors.name: Inflation Pressures (CPI/PPI/PCE)
factors.summary: Fed Governor Michael Barr flagged inflation as 'overwhelming risk'; producer costs accelerating per recent data. Oil prices surging (WTI >$100/bbl) on Iran conflict—direct input cost headwind for Dow industrials and transportation.

factors.name: Oil & Commodity Prices
factors.summary: WTI crude broke above $100/bbl after Trump-Xi summit; Iran conflict unresolved, Strait of Hormuz supply concerns persist. Higher energy costs pressure margins across CAT, BA, CVX and increase CPI/inflation expectations.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: Dollar rallying toward best week since March on hawkish Fed repricing; EUR/USD broke below 1.170 and 200-day MA. USD strength dampens multinational earnings translation for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure (MSFT, BA, CAT, JPM).

factors.name: Trump-Xi Summit Outcome
factors.summary: Summit wrapped Friday with 'constructive' rhetoric but no major trade breakthroughs or Iran resolution. China agreed to buy 200-750 Boeing planes (BA positive), but no commitments on Taiwan or Strait of Hormuz opening. Mixed signal—upside limited, downside hedged.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran Conflict)
factors.summary: Iran war persists, negotiations at stalemate, Strait of Hormuz remains under pressure. Shipping routes shifting (India, Panama Canal), global supply chains disrupted. VIX likely to remain elevated; equity momentum faces headwinds from tail risk.

factors.name: Fed Policy Transition (Powell → Warsh)
factors.summary: Jerome Powell's last day as Fed Chair today; Kevin Warsh takes over amid inflation angst. Market perception is new Fed chairs are 'tested by turmoil'—added uncertainty. No clarity on forward guidance until Warsh's first communication.

factors.name: Stock Market Technicals / Profit Taking
factors.summary: BofA strategists flag stock market 'ripe for profit taking in June'; Nasdaq futures showing bearish reversal, sell-the-news setup. Dow futures fell 0.35% in European hours Friday; move already underway. Momentum crowding into equities now seen as risk.

factors.name: AI-Driven Earnings (Narrow Rally)
factors.summary: BNY notes US equities propelled by AI-led earnings boom outpacing broader economy. Supports large-cap Dow names (MSFT), but rally concentration and macro headwinds may cap upside. Not enough to offset rate/inflation concerns.

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings Pipeline
factors.summary: No major Dow heavyweight earnings in immediate news window; focus shifts to forward guidance from cyclicals (CAT, DE, BA) post-summit. Earnings season tail-end, so limited catalyst flow until next cycle.

factors.name: UK Political Uncertainty
factors.summary: UK PM Keir Starmer facing challengers (possible Andy Burnham), GBP worst week since 2024. Spillover risk to US equities if UK credit/fiscal concerns widen, but secondary to US inflation/Fed narrative.

factors.name: VIX / Credit Spreads
factors.summary: No specific VIX level cited in latest news, but geopolitical tension (Iran, Ukraine spillover) and bond selloff suggest volatility bias higher. Credit spreads likely widening on rate shock; equity risk premium rising.

screening.narrative: US30 is caught in a PHASE 2 pullback after a strong multi-week rally. The dominant macro story has shifted from soft-landing optimism to inflation/rate shock: rising Treasury yields (10Y near 1-year highs, 30Y >5%), surging oil (WTI >$100 on Iran supply fears), and new Fed Chair Warsh's 'first-test' uncertainty are pressing equities. Trump-Xi summit delivered diplomatic warmth but no material breakthroughs on trade or Iran, leaving geopolitical risk premium intact. The bond market is doing the heavy lifting, repricing Fed hike probability upward; equities are now seen as crowded and ripe for profit-taking (BofA call). Dow specifically is pressure-tested by USD strength (hurting multinational translation), energy input cost inflation, and dividend/yield compression from rising real rates.
catalysts_ahead: Kevin Warsh Fed Chair inaugural commentary/testimony (expected within days)
University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment (likely next week)
Initial Jobless Claims (weekly)
PPI/CPI prints if released (early next week)
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (mid-late next week)
Dow component guidance updates (CAT, BA, DE earnings calls if any in next 72h)
Iran negotiations update or Strait of Hormuz escalation
Oil price breakout >$105 or pullback <$98 (key volatility trigger)
screening.risk_warning: Bias would FLIP BULLISH if: (1) Oil prices spike above $110 and then stabilize (markets repricing higher energy-cost risk premium, mean-reversion play); (2) Fed Warsh signals dovish pivot or rate-cut ahead (kill rate shock narrative); (3) Iran-US deal announced, Strait reopens (geopolitical risk off); (4) Earnings data from Dow heavies (UNH, MSFT, JPM) beats big and guides higher (earnings trump macro); (5) Treasury yields roll over sharply (>2% daily drop in 10Y). Bias would strengthen BEARISH if oil exceeds $105, Treasury 10Y yields exceed 5.0%, or Nasdaq breaks below 29,200 (Dow often follows tech-led selloff).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (down 0.12–0.23% intraday, futures down 0.35% in Europe). DO NOT CHASE shorts—wait for pullback/retest to 50,100–50,150 resistance or capitulation low near 49,800 before fresh entry. Bias is weak NEUTRAL→BEARISH, so accumulate shorts on rallies into resistance, not into current weakness. Better risk/reward on next bounce.

factors.name: Treasury Yield Spike (Real Rates Higher)
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yield topped 5.1% (highest in ~1 year); 10-year at 4.56% (+10.1 bps); 2-year at 4.07% (+8.1 bps). Rising real rates pressure dividend-heavy Dow (UNH, KO, JNJ, PG, WMT) and cyclical industrials (CAT, BA, GE, HON).

factors.name: USD Strength & DXY Rally
factors.summary: Dollar Index firming sharply as yields rise; USD/CAD at mid-1.3700s (+0.5% week). Dow has ~50% international revenue; strong USD = translation headwind for multinationals (BA, CAT, MSFT, GE). BoJ intervention risk also limits JPY weakness, reducing carry unwind tailwind.

factors.name: Oil Prices Surging (Risk-Off, Inflation Angst)
factors.summary: July crude +$2.93 to $99.85; June contract $103.99 (+$2.82). Iran conflict + China Trump summit uncertainty driving energy volatility. Input cost inflation for transportation/logistics hurts consumer staples and industrials (CAT transport, HD supply costs). Fed Chair Kevin Warsh seen as hawkish on inflation.

factors.name: Market Risk-Off Turn (Flight to Safety)
factors.summary: Nasdaq down -1.69%, Russell 2000 down -2.0%, S&P 500 down -1.20%. Dow down -0.83% (-415 pts below 50k support). Broad equity selloff suggests sentiment shift from AI euphoria to macro/inflation concern. Risk appetite reversing.

factors.name: NY Fed Manufacturing Index Better-Than-Expected
factors.summary: Empire State Manufacturing index 19.6 vs 7.5 est. (+11 pts MoM); new orders 22.7, shipments 18.9. Pricing power evident: prices paid 62.6 (+11.5 pts), prices received 31.8 (+10). Strong but inflationary—supports rate-hike bias, negating equity tailwind.

factors.name: US Industrial Production Beat (0.7% vs 0.3% est.)
factors.summary: April industrial production +0.7% (vs 0.3% expected); capacity utilization 76.1% vs 75.8% est. Data supports economic resilience but also underpins inflation narrative—Fed likely to hold rates higher longer. Mixed for Dow.

factors.name: Fed Policy Hawkish Shift (Warsh Era)
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh perceived as inflation hawk. TD Securities now projects NO rate cuts in 2026, next FOMC move still a cut but delayed. Longer duration at elevated rates = headwind for Dow dividend payers and rate-sensitive cyclicals (utilities, REITs).

factors.name: Geopolitical Uncertainty (Trump-Xi Summit, Iran)
factors.summary: Trump-Xi meeting concluded with no confirmed trade deals despite PR; both sides dispute what was agreed (Taiwan, tariffs unclear). Iran conflict unresolved; BRICS meeting collapsed without joint Iran position. Supply-chain/tariff risks rising—headwind for BA, CAT, exporters.

factors.name: Cerebras IPO Pop & Tech Reversal
factors.summary: Cerebras surged 89% at open, then pared gains and fell 10% in early trading (profit-taking). Reflects broader tech momentum rollover; chipmakers (ARM -7.2%) dumped as yields spiked. Dow less exposed to semis than Nasdaq, but HP (printer/IT) and related plays soften.

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings Guidance (Mixed)
factors.summary: Starbucks laying off 300 US employees, closing regional offices (margin/confidence signal). Bill Ackman buying MSFT (bullish signal on AI/cloud). Piper Sandler mix of upgrades (Biogen, Schwab) and cuts (Figma, Boot Barn). No consensus shift.

factors.name: Global Bond Selloff (Risk Asset Headwind)
factors.summary: EUR/USD fell to 1.1620 (5-week lows); GBP on worst week since 2024 vs USD. Global yield spike = synchronized equities pressure. Dow has heavy international exposure; currency weakness + bond selloff = double hit.

factors.name: China Economic Uncertainty (Dow-Relevant: CAT, BA, DE)
factors.summary: Trump summit outcome unclear; no confirmed Boeing 200-plane order (market pricing may have front-run). China growth data lagging amid geopolitical tension. CAT (construction equipment) and other industrial exporters at risk if China slowdown confirmed.

factors.name: Inflation Signals Intensifying (CPI/PCE Risk Ahead)
factors.summary: NY Fed pricing power spiking; oil rally; supplies tight (Russia wheat delays, Gulf aluminium shortages). CPI/PCE releases in coming weeks likely to be hot. Market now pricing in sticky inflation = longer hold of elevated rates.

factors.name: Momentum / Positioning Exhaustion
factors.summary: Goldman Sachs warns record stock surge has delivered rare signal; momentum & risk appetite now a 'problem.' Dow broke above 50k (Feb high) just this week; now rolling over. Late-cycle pullback dynamic evident.

screening.narrative: The Dow has shifted from AI-euphoria / risk-on (past 2 weeks) to macro/inflation-driven risk-off within hours of Trump-Xi summit conclusion and Reuters reports of unconfirmed trade deals. Treasury yields spiked sharply (10Y +10.1 bps to 4.56%, 30Y to 5.1% highs), dragging down dividend/cyclical-heavy Dow -0.83% to 49,650. Rising real rates, strong USD headwinds (international revenue exposure), oil surge (+$2.93), and geopolitical uncertainty (Iran, Taiwan ambiguity) have triggered a rotation out of rate-sensitive cyclicals and multinationals. Fed Chair Warsh is perceived as hawkish on inflation; TD Securities now projects zero 2026 rate cuts. The move is in PHASE_2 (underway ~3-4h into US trading), with breadth deteriorating (Nasdaq -1.69%, Russell 2000 -2.0%).
catalysts_ahead: CPI/PCE inflation data (next 1-2 weeks) — likely hot given oil/input prices; could accelerate selloff
FOMC meeting (late May) — market will parse Warsh's inflation stance vs easing expectations
China Economic Data (PMI, growth rates) — confirmation of slowdown could further depress CAT, BA, industrials
Dow component earnings (JPM, GS, UNH, HD, CAT, BA, GE already in or imminently) — guidance on margin pressure & capex restraint
US retail sales, jobless claims (next week) — recession risk gauge; softer data could trigger flight-to-safety bid
Iran conflict escalation or de-escalation news — oil & risk sentiment extremely sensitive
screening.risk_warning: If Trump-Xi summit details emerge showing genuine trade progress / tariff rollback, or if Fed signals imminent rate cuts (dovish pivot by Warsh), or if oil falls sharply (Iran de-escalation), or if upcoming CPI comes in cool, the Dow could reverse sharply upward. Yield spike could be a 'buy the dip' moment for dividend payers if inflation fears prove transitory. Currently overextended to downside in first hours; retest of 49,800–50,000 into month-end could stabilize.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (30–180 min into established selloff); avoid chasing shorts. Consider short entries on retest of 49,850–50,000 resistance, or wait for pullback/consolidation into 49,200–49,400 before re-shorting. Stop above 50,150. If yields stabilize and sentiment steadies, watch for oversold bounce Friday close / Monday open.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Move underway after opening down 415 points; wait for intraday bounce toward 49900-50000 resistance zone before considering fresh shorts.

factors.name: Treasury Yield Surge
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yield topped 5.1%, highest in nearly a year; 10-year up 10.1 bps to 4.56%, 2-year up 8.1 bps to 4.073%. Rising rates pressure rate-sensitive Dow components (financials, utilities, dividend payers) and increase discount rates for valuations.

factors.name: Oil Price Spike
factors.summary: WTI crude surged $2.93 to $99.85 (July contract) on Iran tensions and China oil purchase agreement. Higher energy prices raise input costs for industrials (CAT, MMM, HON) and squeeze consumer discretionary spending, while inflation fears keep Fed hawkish.

factors.name: Hawkish Fed Expectations
factors.summary: Market pricing in no rate cuts for 2026 under new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh per TD Securities; Powell article highlights failed inflation fight. Oil-driven inflation risks strengthen expectations Fed keeps rates elevated longer, negative for equity multiples.

factors.name: Broad Risk-Off Sentiment
factors.summary: Major indices opened sharply lower: Dow -415 (-0.83%), S&P -1.20%, Nasdaq -1.69%, Russell 2000 -2.0%. Goldman Sachs notes rare momentum signal suggests overextension. VIX likely rising, credit spreads widening in classic risk-off move.

factors.name: Tech Sector Weakness
factors.summary: Cerebras stock fell 10% post-IPO, ARM down 7.2%, chipmakers dumped broadly per Bloomberg. While Dow less tech-heavy than Nasdaq, correlation in risk appetite hurts MSFT (Dow component) and broad sentiment.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: Dollar rallying across the board: EUR/USD below 1.1628 (50% retracement, five-week low), USD/CAD mid-1.37s. Stronger USD creates translation headwinds for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure and signals risk aversion.

factors.name: China Summit Uncertainty
factors.summary: Trump-Xi talks concluded with contradictory claims: US touted trade deals, China emphasized Taiwan warnings, neither confirmed other's claims per Al Jazeera. Trump made no commitment on Taiwan defense. Uncertainty weighs on Boeing (200-750 plane order unclear) and China-exposed industrials (CAT).

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: Iran conflict remains at stalemate per headlines; Trump willing to accept 20-year nuclear suspension but mentions destroying power plants. Strait of Hormuz disruption risk keeps oil elevated and global supply chains uncertain—direct headwind for Dow industrials.

factors.name: Industrial Production Beat
factors.summary: US Industrial Production rose 0.7% in April vs 0.3% expected, with capacity utilization at 76.1% vs 75.8% estimate and manufacturing output up 0.6% vs 0.2% expected. Positive for Dow's industrial base but overshadowed by macro headwinds.

factors.name: NY Fed Manufacturing Surge
factors.summary: Empire State Manufacturing Index jumped to 19.6 in May vs 7.5 expected (prior 11.0), with new orders at 22.7 and delivery times extending. Strong regional manufacturing data supports industrial names but also flags inflation pressures (prices paid 62.6 vs 51.1 prior).

factors.name: Inflation Pressures Embedded
factors.summary: Empire survey shows prices paid surged to 62.6 from 51.1, prices received up to 31.8 from 21.8. Combined with oil spike, reinforces stagflationary fears—input cost squeeze for industrials with limited pricing power.

factors.name: Corporate Actions
factors.summary: Starbucks (not Dow but consumer proxy) cutting 300 jobs; Trump disclosed buying Boeing and Nvidia shares (bullish signal for BA, a Dow component). Ackman built MSFT position citing AI/cloud growth. Mixed signals on corporate confidence.

confirmation.narrative: Dow broke decisively below the psychologically critical 50,000 level (first close above since Feb 11) as a confluence of macro headwinds—surging oil prices, spiking Treasury yields, hawkish Fed repricing, and risk-off flows—overwhelmed robust industrial production data. The 30-year yield hitting 5.1% (near one-year high) is particularly damaging to the Dow's dividend-heavy, rate-sensitive composition. While manufacturing data (IP +0.7%, Empire 19.6) signals real-economy strength, the market is focused on second-order effects: oil-driven inflation preventing Fed cuts, rising input costs squeezing margins, and USD strength hurting multinationals. The Trump-Xi summit ended with ambiguity rather than clarity, leaving China trade and Taiwan risks unresolved.
catalysts_ahead: May PCE inflation data (key Fed decision input)
Next FOMC meeting under Chair Warsh (policy direction)
Dow component earnings (UNH, GS, CAT guidance on margins/demand)
China PMI releases (demand signal for industrials)
Iran conflict developments (oil price direction)
US Retail Sales next release (consumer health check)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if oil reverses sharply below $95 on Iran de-escalation news, or if Treasury yields fall 15+ bps on flight-to-quality, or if Fed Chair Warsh signals surprise dovish pivot. Reclaim and hold above 50,000 with volume would signal false breakdown and flip bias neutral-to-bullish. Watch for coordinated central bank intervention on inflation concerns.

reason: FLIP + CONFIRMED: NEUTRAL → SELL, both models conf ≥7

factors.name: Treasury Yield Surge (Real Rates Higher)
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yield topped 5.1%, highest in nearly a year (Bloomberg 14:55). Global bond selloff deepening on inflation fears and rising oil prices; yields now at multi-decade highs, pressuring rate-sensitive Dow heavyweights (UNH, HD, utilities).

factors.name: Inflation Expectations / Q2 CPI Projection
factors.summary: Top forecasters project inflation to hit 6% in Q2 (MarketWatch 14:20). Recent hotter-than-expected US inflation data (mentioned in CAD/USD context) reinforcing hawkish Fed repricing and longer-duration rate pressure.

factors.name: Fed Policy Uncertainty Under Kevin Warsh
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh confirmed as new Fed chair; market repricing for potential hikes despite 'unhinged' yield environment per SocGen (Bloomberg 14:08). Warsh faces 5 major tests (MarketWatch 14:40), creating policy uncertainty headwinds for equities.

factors.name: Semiconductor Sector Weakness
factors.summary: NVDA -3.70%, MU -5.15%, INTC -6.00% today (Investinglive 14:46). Semiconductor slide marks broad tech resilience deterioration; Dow doesn't hold large semicon but linked to cyclical and industrial sentiment.

factors.name: Global Risk-Off / European Equity Selloff
factors.summary: European indices down -1% to -2% on day (DAX -2.07%, CAC -1.6%, FTSE -1.71%). Broad global deleveraging tide; UK political turmoil (Starmer leadership race per Al Jazeera 13:59) adds geopolitical friction.

factors.name: China Trade Deal Disappointment
factors.summary: Trump-Xi Beijing summit ended with no major trade breakthroughs confirmed (BBC 15:03). Both sides disputed what was agreed; vague Boeing deal (200–750 jets) not sufficient to offset broader risk-off and tariff/trade policy uncertainty headwinds.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Hawkish Repricing
factors.summary: USD strengthening on hawkish Fed repricing (ING 13:51, FXStreet 14:49). Stronger dollar pressures multinational earnings translation for Dow (50% int'l exposure), particularly industrials and aerospace (BA, CAT).

factors.name: Oil Prices / Energy Cost Pressures
factors.summary: Rising oil prices cited as spooking bond investors (Bloomberg 16:44); Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz blockade persist. Input cost inflation for transportation and manufacturing Dow names (UPS, CAT, MMM) remains elevated.

factors.name: Market Breadth Deterioration
factors.summary: Major indices open sharply lower: S&P -1.20%, NASDAQ -1.69%, Russell 2000 -2.0% (Investinglive 13:37). Dow -0.83% at open but lagging rally in MSFT (+2.43% on Ackman backing) masks broader weak breadth.

factors.name: Rate-Sensitive Dividend Pressure
factors.summary: Rising long-end yields (5%+ on 30y) eroding total return appeal of Dow dividend payers (KO, JNJ, PG, WMT). Dividend discount model compression as discount rates rise weighs on valuations.

factors.name: Seasonal / Technical Positioning
factors.summary: Dow briefly surpassed 50k last week; now pullback below that level on Friday suggests profit-taking and technical resistance. No major seasonal headwind noted in this window.

factors.name: AI Disruption Narrative / Workforce Concerns
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports AI-driven workforce displacement (15:45); family offices shunning AI for old-economy plays. Sentiment shift away from mega-cap tech to value/industrial, but Dow exposure is mixed.

screening.narrative: US30 opened lower Friday (−0.83%) in the wake of a broad global equity selloff triggered by surging Treasury yields (30y at 5.1%, highest in ~1 year) and elevated inflation expectations (Q2 CPI forecast at 6%). Trump's Beijing summit ended with no confirmed trade breakthroughs, removing a key risk-off hedge. The Fed repricing cycle under new Chair Kevin Warsh is now the dominant headwind: hawkish expectations, rising real rates, and USD strength are all pressuring rate-sensitive Dow names (dividend payers, utilities, financials) and multinational translation. European indices fell 1–2%, and breadth deterioration (NASDAQ −1.69%, Russell −2.0%) signals the selloff is broad, not isolated to tech. The semiconductor sector's sharp decline (INTC −6%, NVDA −3.70%, MU −5.15%) adds to sentiment weakness, though Dow exposure is less direct.
catalysts_ahead: ISM Manufacturing PMI (next week expected) — direct Dow industrial bellwether
Fed speakers & policy comments under Warsh (ongoing) — rate expectations
Durable goods orders, factory orders, industrial production (next release window) — cyclical read
Dow heavyweight earnings updates (UNH, GS, JPM, BA, CAT) — individual stock movers
Treasury yield trajectory & auction calendar (next 2–3 days) — refinance cycle signal
China PMI and growth data (mid/late week) — CAT, BA, industrials demand signal
Oil prices and Strait of Hormuz status updates (daily monitors) — energy input costs
screening.risk_warning: Move is already in progress (Phase 2); if Fed rhetoric shifts dovish or trade deal clarity emerges, yields could correct lower and equities rebound. If inflation data surprises cool (deflation fears) and bond market stabilizes, Dow could reclaim 50k+ quickly. Conversely, if Q2 CPI surprise higher or Warsh signals aggressive tightening, downside to 48500–49000 becomes likely.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway and has already driven Dow −0.83% at open; do not chase shorts aggressively. Wait for a retest of 50000–50200 resistance for a fresh short entry, or if support breaks at 49000–49200, consider adds on a pullback into that zone. Avoid chasing 4h gains; focus on risk/reward at key levels.

factors.name: US Inflation Surge & Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: Economic forecasters project inflation hitting 6% in Q2 2026 (source: Al Jazeera); fed funds futures now pricing in a hike as soon as December (MarketWatch). This is a sharp hawkish repricing that pressures equities and particularly rate-sensitive dividend payers in the Dow.

factors.name: 10Y & 30Y Treasury Yields Spike
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yield topped 5.1%, highest in nearly a year and highest since 2007 levels (Bloomberg/MarketWatch). This duration-driven selloff impacts dividend-heavy Dow names (utilities, financials) and increases discount rates on future cash flows.

factors.name: Global Bond Selloff & Yield Contagion
factors.summary: European indices closed sharply lower (-1% to -2.07%) on global bond selloff; German DAX -2.07%, France CAC -1.6%, UK FTSE -1.71% (Investinglive). This synchronized de-risking suggests macro uncertainty and reduced appetite for equities globally.

factors.name: China Summit Disappointment & No Trade Breakthrough
factors.summary: Trump's two-day China visit yielded no confirmed major trade deals; Strait of Hormuz blockade remains unresolved, keeping oil supply risks elevated (Bloomberg). Boeing got a vague order, but 'little clarity on numbers, timing, or aircraft type.' Dow has ~50% international revenue, CAT and BA heavily dependent on China growth signals.

factors.name: Oil Price Rally & Inflation Pressure
factors.summary: WTI broke above $100/bbl (trading ~$100.90, up 3.13% on day) on Hormuz supply fears and war premium (FXStreet). Higher energy costs feed through to industrial input costs and transportation, pressuring Dow industrials' margins; geopolitical risk remains high.

factors.name: Semiconductor Sector Weakness
factors.summary: Nvidia -3.70%, Micron -5.15%, Intel -6.00% on China disappointment and AI exuberance fade (Investinglive). Semiconductors are part of broader tech rotation; weakness in chip stocks signals investor unease about forward earnings in a high-rate environment.

factors.name: USD Strength & DXY Rally
factors.summary: EUR/USD sank to one-month lows; GBP/USD down >2% for the week on UK political turmoil (FXStreet). Strong dollar pressures Dow's multinational earnings translation and makes US exports less competitive, especially critical for CAT, BA, GE, MMM.

factors.name: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Taking Helm
factors.summary: Warsh's appointment signals potential hawkish tilt on inflation control; market is already pricing in near-term rate hikes. New Fed chair typically adds uncertainty but is not yet a fresh catalyst (more of a backdrop).

factors.name: Credit Spreads Holding Firm
factors.summary: Corporate bonds remain bid despite geopolitical risks; credit investors shrugging off Middle East conflict and buying on blue-chip strength (Bloomberg). This suggests institutional conviction in large-cap earnings is not completely broken, a mild offsetting signal.

factors.name: Bill Ackman Buying Microsoft
factors.summary: MSFT up ~2.43% on report of Pershing Square stake (Investinglive). MSFT is a Dow heavyweight (price-weighted); single large institutional bets on mega-cap tech can provide near-term support to the index.

factors.name: Recent Price Action - Minimal Intraday Move
factors.summary: 4h change -0.00%, 24h change -0.13%. This flat action suggests the market has already priced in much of the week's macro moves (China, yields, inflation) and is in consolidation mode, waiting for Monday data or next catalyst.

factors.name: Bias History Consistency
factors.summary: Prior reads on 2026-05-15: NEUTRAL→SELL (14:05, Haiku→Sonnet confirmation)→SELL (16:51, no confirmation). This is now the third consecutive read with a SELL lean, indicating sustained downside bias rather than a whipsaw.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between a hawkish inflation surprise (Q2 forecast at 6%), surging long-end Treasury yields (30Y at 5.1%, 2007 highs), and a failed China trade summit that left crude oil elevated (~$100.91/bbl) with no resolution on the Strait of Hormuz blockade. European equities sold off sharply (-1% to -2%), and the USD rallied hard against EUR and GBP, both headwinds for Dow multinationals (CAT, BA, GE, MMM). While MSFT received a boost from Ackman buying and credit spreads remain anchored, the macro backdrop is heavily BEARISH: rate hike expectations have shifted forward, dividend payers face higher discount rates, and industrial input costs (oil, transport) are climbing into a soft-growth environment. The market's flatness in the last 4h (no net move) suggests consolidation ahead of Monday's TIC data and housing market index, but the bias remains tilted lower on macro deterioration.
catalysts_ahead: TIC data (Monday, May 19) – foreign investment flows into US Treasuries; key for understanding yield support
Housing Market Index (Monday) – real estate weakness would reinforce soft landing skepticism
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (early-mid week) – direct read on Dow's cyclical exposure
Fed speakers (Warsh, others) – any commentary on inflation path or rate trajectory could re-accelerate yields
Dow component earnings revisions – CAT, BA, GE forward guidance critical as oil/rates pressure margins
China economic data (if released) – PMI, industrial production, any reopening signals
Geopolitical escalation (Iran, Taiwan, Middle East) – could further widen credit spreads and boost safe-haven demand, keeping yields elevated
screening.risk_warning: If Monday's data (TIC, housing) prints soft and markets conclude Fed will cut before hiking, yields could collapse and Dow could rebound sharply, especially if tech (MSFT, AAPL earnings season) surprises on the upside. Additionally, a major China deal announcement (delayed from summit) or sudden de-escalation in Middle East tensions could trigger a sharp reversal. The index is also technically near psychological 50k, which is a magnet for mean-reversion longs.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early-phase deterioration (Phase 2) with confirmation from prior day's SELL reads. Avoid chasing shorts into flat price action; wait for a retest of 49,700–49,800 resistance or a break below 49,200 support for conviction entries. If yields continue to spike on Monday's data, consider shorts on retest of intraday highs. Watch credit spreads and equity put/call ratios for capitulation signals before shorting further.
